commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The righteous perishes, and no man lays it to heart: and merciful men are taken away, none considering that the righteous is taken away from the evil to come. Christ, (Calmet) Josias, (Grotius) or any whose cause is just, yet finds no protection from such corrupt magistrates. (Haydock) Evil, by the wicked, or to prevent his fall, 4 Kings xii. People little consider what a loss the world sustains, when those die who might have averted the divine wrath. (Calmet) They are usually taken away, that they may not witness such misfortunes, and are settled in eternal peace. (Worthington).
